Now there are several new programs.
Emmanuele, thank you. So, how many patients can take this treatment in Brazil?
The patient population is large, and it is growing. We have a lot of patients who can start the treatment this year.
Good. And how much evidence do we have for the higher dose?
A lot of new evidence, and more data every quarter. The dose is safe.
And the access? How much access is there in the public sector?
Two years ago there used to be almost no access. Today there is more: several new programs give better coverage in the private sector. But there is still not much public funding.
Thank you. That is exactly the picture I needed. Are there any updates on the reimbursement?
Not yet. The decision is slow. On Thursday I would like to discuss the public funding with you. So far, so good.
The patient population is large, and it is growing.
We have a lot of new evidence for the higher dose.
There are several new programs in the private sector.
There is not much public funding yet.
Two years ago there used to be almost no access.

Emmanuele, Marc Dubois here. Before the business review on Thursday, I want you ready for three data questions, because Global always asks them. First: how many patients can take this treatment in Brazil? I want the patient population, more or less. Second: how much access is there today, in the public sector and the private sector? And third: how much evidence do we have for the new dose? Do not give me a hundred numbers. Give me a few numbers that matter, and one sentence for each. I remember two years ago there used to be almost no access in the public sector. I want to know what changed. Talk to you Thursday.

Good morning, everyone. Claire Bennett, with the market access update from the global immunology team. First, the good news. The patient population for the dermatology treatment is large, and it is growing in several countries. There is a lot of new evidence for the higher dose, and funding in the private sector is stable. Now the difficult part. There used to be very little access in the public sector, and today there is still not much. We have a few programs under review, but the reimbursement decision is slow. So, before Thursday, please send me two numbers: how many patients your team is treating this quarter, and how much public funding you have secured. Thank you, everyone.

Good morning, Marc. Here is the market access update for Brazil. The patient population is large, and it is growing. We have a lot of new evidence for the higher dose. But there is not much access in the public sector yet, and funding is slow. Two years ago there used to be almost none, so this is real progress.
